entry.title: 1H 2026 SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors: Connected Solutions and New AI-First Capabilities
entry.content:
With the 1H 2026 release, we have delivered new capabilities within SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors to improve how organizations intelligently attract and hire the best talent.  

To learn more watch the on-demand Q&A webinars: 1H 2026 Product Release Webinars and Recruiting & Onboarding 1H 2026 Customer Release Q&A. To hear more about the roadmap and broader view of our strategy, watch the 1H 2026 VIP Virtual Program for Strategic Roadmap Updates.    

All of the SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 release items can be found on the What’s New Viewer. These new features will be live in Production on May 15th, 2026.

With the 1H 2026 release, we have delivered a deeper integration between SAP SuccessFactors HCM and SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors as well as updates to Winston, SmartRecruiters’ suite of AI products, which are now available for SAP SuccessFactors customers.

Our integration with SmartRecruiters allows for more consistency and synergy across both products and allows our customers to take advantage of the latest recruiting AI in candidate conversion and team engagement.

Now let’s explore the new features, including a deeper integration between SAP SuccessFactors and SmartRecruiters as well as updates to Winston products such as Companion, Chat, and Match.

SmartRecruiters & SAP SuccessFactors Integration

SmartRecruiters and SAP SuccessFactors now have a native integration with HCM sync and SSO login. That means a seamless, unified experience across both ecosystems with easy connection to Employee Central and Onboarding modules.

With these solutions connected, you can look forward to greater operational efficiency that eliminates the need to manually enter data or replicate work.

SmartRecruiters Winston for SAP SuccessFactors Launch

SmartRecruiters Winston for SAP SuccessFactors is now fully available for SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting customers, bringing conversational AI capabilities into your current solution to elevate candidate engagement and experiences.

SmartRecruiters Winston for SAP SuccessFactors runs on your career site to help recruiting teams engage candidates at scale with generative AI, from job discovery and applications to screening and interview scheduling.

The entire candidate journey is delivered conversationally, so every action is as easy as sending a message to a friend. For example, a candidate looking for a job could simply ask Winston ‘do you have any sales jobs in Seattle?’ and receive an answer directly in the chat.

Look forward to boosting candidate conversion and reducing administrative work with these feature highlights:

  • Chat available 24/7
  • Customizable avatar, colors, and tone of voice
  • Conversational job discovery, applications, and screening questions
  • Automated interview scheduling based on criteria like answers to screening questions.
  • Dashboard to review active bots and performance metrics

Winston Companion: Q&A Agent

Delivered within the core SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors solution, customers benefit from Winston’s enhanced AI capabilities that streamline recruiting processes and drive better business outcomes. Winston Companion’s Q&A agent is your hiring team’s one-stop shop for instant, trustworthy recruiting system and process answers. Users can ask questions without leaving their workflow, rather than opening new tabs and manually looking through article after article.

This convenient new feature helps recruiters save time and stay on task with the following feature highlights:

  • Ask questions about the recruiting process or system directly in Winston Companion.
  • Get instant, contextualized answers with link references to ensure accuracy and allow you to dive deeper.

Winston Companion: Auto Self-Scheduling

Winston Companion’s Auto Self-Scheduling gives hirers a fast, low‑touch way to move qualified candidates from shortlist to confirmed interviews, without the usual back‑and‑forth over email or chat.

By combining preconfigured interview templates, calendar availability, and automation rules, Winston Companion can trigger one‑click scheduling flows directly from a conversational prompt, whether a hirer is working in the web app or via mobile channels like WhatsApp.

Look forward to increased efficiency with the following key features:

  • Template‑driven automation that uses existing interview templates and self-scheduling rules to send out accurate, consistent invites
  • Bulk invitations for high‑volume roles

Winston Match: Subscores

Winston Match subscores give recruiters a holistic, nuanced view of how each candidate’s skills, experience, and education contribute to their overall Match Score. Instead of a single aggregate number, subscores expose the underlying signals Winston uses to evaluate fit, so teams can see not just who is ranked highly, but why they’re ranked that way.

For high-volume screening, subscores make Winston Match insights instantly usable in list views. This accelerates shortlisting while keeping recruiters firmly in control of who moves forward through the following key features.

  • Subscore dimensions of skills, experience, and education/certifications
  • Easy overview of subscores in the applicant list
  • Flexible UI allows users to hide the subscore column if not needed

SmartRecruiters Winston for SAP SuccessFactors: Search Jobs via Resume with Winston Chat

SmartRecruiters Winston for SAP SuccessFactors: Search Jobs via Resume makes job discovery as simple as dropping a CV into the conversation through Winston Chat. Instead of typing keywords or scrolling through pages of listings, candidates upload their resume and let Winston Chat surface relevant roles automatically.

Enable recruiters to reduce friction in the application process while also matching candidates to the most relevant roles, so you can look forward to a boost in high-quality candidate conversion.

Continuous Innovation

At SAP, our goal with each release is to make the recruiting process more efficient and consistent while adding a touch of joy along the way. We want to empower hirers to take time for strategic tasks and meaningful communication that makes your team more visible and valued.

That’s why we are so excited about this round of updates. With the enhanced suite of Winston capabilities, recruiters can leverage generative AI directly within their workflow to boost the metrics that matter to them, like candidate conversion and time to hire, whether that means leveraging Winston Match subscores or SmartRecruiters Winston for SAP SuccessFactors automated scheduling.

With every release, we are excited to deliver these new innovations and continue our journey of providing even greater value to our customers. For full details, please check out the 1H 2026 Release Highlights Resources: 

  • 1H 2026 Release Highlights Brochure
  • 1H 2026 Release Highlights Video 
  • Product Release & Road Map Information

Learn more about SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors.

entry.title: Automating Probation Extensions After Sick Leave in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
entry.content:
Introduction

In many organizations, the probation period is designed to give both the employer and the employee a fair window to assess performance, cultural fit, and overall suitability for the role. But what happens when that evaluation period is interrupted? Employee absences, especially due to short term sickness, can significantly reduce the time available for meaningful observation, leaving managers with incomplete information at the end of the probation timeline.

To address this, some companies adopt a policy of extending the probation period by the exact number of days an employee was absent during that time. While the intent is straightforward, the execution can quickly become complex. Tracking absence days, recalculating end dates, and ensuring consistency across the organization often leads to manual effort, errors, and compliance risks.

This is where SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central (EC) can play a crucial role. By leveraging automation and business rules, organizations can seamlessly adjust probation end dates based on recorded absences, ensuring fairness, accuracy, and operational efficiency. In this blog, we’ll explore how to design and implement an automated approach to extending probation periods when short term sick leave occurs, helping HR teams move from manual tracking to a more intelligent, system-driven process.

Proposed Solution

The proposed solution is based on standard SuccessFactors functionalities, namely Intelligent Services Center (ISC), Integration Center (IC), and Business Rules.

In this scenario, whenever a short-term sickness absence is recorded, an Employee Time event triggers a process to evaluate whether the employee is still within the probation period and, if so, automatically extend the probation end date in the Job Information portlet.

Below is a step-by-step guide to building it.

Step 1: Navigate to Integration Center and click “My Integrations” to start creating your IC job.

Step 2: Now click on “+ Create” and select “More Integration Types”.

Step 3: In this step, you need to define the integration type. For this scenario, select the options as shown in the screenshot below. Once selected, click “Create.”

Step 4: On the first screen of the IC job, you are required to provide a name, a description, and a page size. Below is an example from a demo system. Once these fields are defined, click “Next.”

Step 5: Next, navigate to the “Configure Fields” tab and, from the menu in the top-right corner, select the “Switch to Field Mapping View” option.

On this page, we first define the destination table, for this scenario, we use “Job Information.”

Next, we configure the mapping from the “Employee Time” table to Job Information. As shown in the screenshot below, only two fields are directly mapped: “Event Date,” which is mapped to the absence “End Date,” and “User ID,” mapped to the “User ID” from the source table.

These mappings are created easily by dragging and dropping fields from the source table on the left to the corresponding fields in the destination table on the right.

Step 6: Next, switch to “Detail View” using the option in the top-right menu.

We will now define fields that do not have a direct mapping but instead require a default value. To do this, locate the “Sequence Number” field in the table on the right. Select it, then in the left panel assign a value greater than 2, for example, 9.

Setting a number higher than 2 helps prevent potential errors. If, on the same day, the job attempts to create a new time slice in Job Information while another record already exists, the system will use this higher value to generate an additional sequence number. It’s important to note that the final sequence may not necessarily be 9, as the system intelligently determines the correct sequence based on existing records.

Now we complete the second action in this step by assigning a default value to the “Event Reason” field for this scenario.

Note: It is recommended to create a dedicated event reason for this use case to avoid unintentionally triggering the business rule (which we will configure later) in other situations.

Step 7: We have now completed the creation of our IC job, so click “Save” as shown in the screenshot.

Step 8: In this step, we will assign the IC job we created to the ISC. Navigate to the Intelligent Services Center page, search for the event “Employee Short Term Disability,” and click to open it.

Step 9: Create a new flow if one already exists for this event. Click “Integration” from the right-hand menu, which will add a new line in the center of the page. Set the “Timing” to “When event is published.” Then, use the three-dot menu (highlighted in green) to save your changes.

Note: It is also advisable to create an ISC rule that limits the process to employees whose probation status is neither completed nor failed (or based on any other criteria relevant to your organization).

Step 10: In this step, we will create our first business rule, which will trigger the Intelligent Services Center event. To do this, navigate to the “Configure Object Definition” transaction and select the “Employee Time” object.

Click to create a new rule, following the example below. Once the rule is created, assign it on Save in the object and then save the object.

Note: The absence type used in the rule example is for demonstration purposes only. Please ensure you use the absence type relevant to your organization.

Step 11: Our final step is to create a business rule that calculates the new probation end date considering the number of days the employee was on sick leave.

To do this, navigate to “Manage Business Configuration” > “Job Information.” In edit mode, scroll to the bottom of the page. On the last line, click to create a new business rule as shown below. Once the rule is created, assign it to the onSave event in the portlet and save your changes.

Conclusion

By combining Intelligent Services Center (ISC), Integration Center (IC), and Business Rules, we have demonstrated how SAP SuccessFactors can be configured to automate the extension of probation periods based on employee sick leave. This approach ensures that any absence occurring during the probation period is accurately reflected by extending the end date accordingly, eliminating the need for manual adjustments.

Beyond improving administrative efficiency, this solution also enhances fairness and consistency in HR processes, ensuring that all employees are evaluated over an equivalent active working period. It reduces the risk of human error, strengthens compliance with internal policies, and provides a scalable framework that can be adapted to other absence-driven scenarios.

Finally, this use case illustrates the power of SAP SuccessFactors standard tools when combined thoughtfully. With the right configuration, organizations can transform complex HR requirements into automated, transparent, and maintainable processes that support both HR teams and employees alike.

I hope this solution helps you improve your Employee Central setup. Feel free to reach out or comment if you have questions, or if you’d like to share how you’ve tackled probation extensions due to employees absences in your own environment.

entry.title: Turning “Moments of truth” into lasting impressions with SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding
entry.content:
"Moments of truth" in Onboarding

Admittedly, it has been a while since I studied Service Management at business school, and many of the theories date back to the 1980s. Still, one idea has always stayed with me: people judge experiences by comparing their expectations with their perception. In short, this is how we assess an experience. These are often referred to as “Moments of Truth.”

Even today, this concept remains highly relevant, if you ask me.

In the employee journey, one of the most important moments (of truth) happens after the contract is signed, when preboarding and onboarding begin. This is where expectations are tested, and first impressions are formed through communication, preparation, and early interactions.

It is both emotional and practical, and these first impressions tend to last.

Across industries, a common pattern is that organizations invest heavily in attracting talent, but far less in structuring the onboarding experience from “offer signed” to day one. People and relationships remain essential, but without structure and process support, the experience often becomes inconsistent.

This is where SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding can support organizations by enabling a more consistent, structured, and scalable onboarding experience. If it is used to its full extend.

 

From candidate to employee - without friction

At its core, SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding connects what is typically disconnected. It brings together systems, processes, and people into one experience.

Instead of handing a new hire over from recruiting into emails, PDFs, and spreadsheets, everything continues in one flow. The candidate becomes a new hire, and then an employee, without changing platform or experience.

The solution supports onboarding, cross-boarding, offboarding, and rehire scenarios in one place. This reflects the reality that employee journeys are not always linear.

In many implementations, the value of having one centralized experience is initially underestimated. In practice, it significantly reduces friction and manual coordination.

 

A guided, structured onboarding experience that sets people up for success

The system is designed around guided processes. New hires are not expected to figure things out themselves. Instead, they are supported step by step through tasks such as data collection, document handling, learning, and introductions.


 

Before day one, employees can access a personalized homepage to complete forms and prepare. On day one, the focus shifts to connections, clarity, and productivity. Activities such as buddy assignments, meetings, and onboarding tasks are already planned.

This creates a more consistent onboarding experience while still allowing flexibility for different roles and local requirements.

From a process perspective, this reduces waiting time and helps employees become productive earlier.

 

Seamless onboarding and data flow across the SuccessFactors suite

One of the most powerful aspects of SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding is its native connection to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central.

This is not a traditional integration. Data entered during onboarding is written directly into Employee Central. There is no duplication, no interfaces, and no need to re-enter information. From day one, you have one consistent and reliable data foundation.

What makes this particularly strong is that it applies to all actors involved in the process. The new hire, even before officially starting and while still an external user, can access a secure part of the system and enter their own data directly into the core HR system. The same applies to hiring managers and HR, who can update or enrich data during onboarding. Everything is written to the same place, in real time.

This has a clear practical impact. It reduces errors, removes manual steps, and ensures that employee data is available immediately for downstream processes.

Building on this foundation, onboarding also connects to the broader HR landscape. Recruiting triggers onboarding, Learning can assign training, and Performance can support early goal setting.

In many organizations, these processes are still handled separately, and expectations for the first days are often loosely defined and depend on individual managers.

By linking onboarding to structured plans such as 30-60-90 day goals, organizations can define expectations early, assign learning before day one, and track progress from the start.

This is where onboarding moves beyond administration and becomes part of broader workforce enablement.

 

Bringing structure and control to onboarding

From an HR and manager perspective, the value is not only in the experience, but also in the control.

Dashboards provide visibility into tasks, deadlines, and overdue activities. Tasks can be triggered automatically, reminders can be sent, and bottlenecks can be identified early.



What was previously handled through manual follow-ups becomes structured workflows.

This is particularly relevant in multi-country environments, where consistency and compliance are important.

The system allows configuration of business rules, automation of processes, and integration with the broader HR landscape through APIs. This means onboarding becomes part of the overall HR architecture rather than a standalone process.

 

What value does this actually create?

If we move beyond system terminology, the value can be summarized in a few key outcomes:

  • New hires arrive better prepared and ready to contribute from day one
  • Managers gain clarity on responsibilities and timelines
  • HR achieves greater consistency and visibility across teams and locations
  • Time to productivity is reduced through better preparation and alignment
  • Onboarding becomes structured and measurable rather than ad hoc

And importantly:

  • Organizations improve their ability to deliver a consistent first impression

Because onboarding is not just an HR activity. It is a key moment where expectations are either confirmed or challenged.



Closing thoughts

Many organizations focus heavily on attracting talent, but less on what happens immediately after.

If onboarding still relies on emails, documents, and manual follow-ups, it may be worth reviewing how the process is structured.

Improving onboarding is not only about efficiency. It is about shaping the employee experience at a critical moment.

 

Jonas Kyhnau is a SAP SuccessFactors Senior Consultant working at Pentos Denmark.
I help companies optimising and streamlining their core HR processes with practical, scalable solutions. I cover all process areas from Recruiting (including SmartRecruiters of course) through Onboarding to Core HR in Employee Central.

SAP SuccessFactors Platform SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding

#Pentos - SAP SuccessFactors Partner Danmark

entry.title: How We Built JUNO Joule Agent for Release Testing Automation Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM
entry.content:
Introduction

From my experience working on multiple SAP SuccessFactors release cycles, one thing became very clear early on — automation alone is not enough. What really makes a difference is how intelligently we manage and execute that automation.

In most projects, teams already invest a lot of effort in building automated test scenarios using internal frameworks or QA tools. But the real challenge starts during release time — deciding what to run, when to run it, and how to interpret the results.

I’ve personally seen teams struggle with simple but critical things like:

  • Manually triggering test executions
  • Switching between tools to track execution status
  • Trying to understand raw test results
  • Mapping technical failures back to business impact

Even though automation exists, the process still feels manual and fragmented.

This gap is exactly what led us to build JUNO Joule Agent — to make release testing not just automated, but also intelligent and easy to manage.    

Current Challenge

In most SAP SuccessFactors implementations I’ve worked on, organizations already have:

  • Automated test scenarios built in their release testing frameworks
  • CI/CD pipelines capable of triggering executions

But what’s usually missing is:

  • A simple, business-friendly way to trigger tests
  • Smart selection of relevant test scenarios per release
  • Clear visibility into execution results
  • A guided or conversational way to validate releases

Because of this, even though testing is technically automated, the overall process still depends heavily on manual effort.

Our Approach – JUNO Joule Agent

To solve this problem, we designed JUNO Joule Agent, an AI-powered release testing solution built on SAP Business Technology Platform.

While working on this, my main focus was to ensure the solution feels natural within the SAP ecosystem — secure, scalable, and easy for both technical and business users.

As part of the implementation, we built the solution using SAP Build Process Automation, leveraging Joule Actions, Skills, and Agent capabilities to orchestrate the entire release testing flow. This helped us connect user requests directly with backend automation in a seamless way.

Instead of creating new test scripts again, JUNO reuses existing automated test scenarios from the release testing framework and brings everything into one place.

With this approach, teams can trigger test execution using simple natural language, monitor execution in real time, and analyze results without switching across multiple tools.

It basically adds an intelligent layer on top of existing automation, which is what was missing earlier.

Benefits

1. Centralized Test Dashboard

  • Displays a unified dashboard to execute and monitor test scenarios, along with options to export results.
  • Provides visibility into test scenarios and execution status across different modules.

 

 Figure 1: JUNO Joule Agent showing list of configured Joule Skills, Agents and release testing artifacts

 

2. Intelligent Test Execution via Joule

  • Accepts natural language user requests and identifies the corresponding release test scenario.

  • Triggers test execution directly from the conversation and provides execution summary and monitoring details.

 

Figure 2:  Natural Language-Based Test Execution using Joule with real-time execution summary and monitoring details

 

Figure 3: Real-time test result insights using Joule showing passed and failed scenario counts

 

3. Faster and More Efficient Testing

  • From what I observed during implementation, this significantly reduced repetitive manual testing effort and helped teams complete release cycles faster with fewer errors.

  • It also allowed HR and IT teams to focus more on analysis and improvements instead of spending time on manual execution.

Figure 4: Triggering multiple release test scenarios using Joule with execution status and monitoring details

 

4. Release Highlights Available in the UI

  • Displays SAP SuccessFactors release updates directly within the application.

  • Shows key changes and related information in a structured format for quick reference.

 5. Testing Across SAP SuccessFactors HCM Modules

  • Supports release testing across multiple SAP SuccessFactors modules, including Employee Central, Onboarding, and Recruiting.

  • Retrieves available test scenarios across these modules using Joule.

 

Figure 5:  Conversational retrieval of Employee Central data using Joule with real-time response display

 

Figure 6: Detailed view of Time Off test cases and execution insights retrieved through Joule conversational interface

 

Figure 7: Conversational view of release RCM test cases and associated scenario details using Joule

6. Test Scenario Execution Using Inputs

  • Allows users to run test scenarios for a specific Entity ID instead of executing all scenarios.

  • Executes only the relevant test cases based on the provided input.

 

Figure 8: Input-driven hierarchical test scenario execution using Joule with detailed execution summary

 

Figure 9: Triggering release test scenarios using Joule with execution success status and details

 7. Release Test Coverage Across SAP SuccessFactors Modules

  • Displays the number of automated test scenarios available for each SAP SuccessFactors HCM module.
  • Retrieves test scenario counts across modules using Joule.

 

Figure 10: Conversational retrieval of test scenario counts across all SAP SuccessFactors modules using Joule

 

Figure 11: Real-time insight into average execution time of release test scenarios using Joule

 

Business Value

From my hands-on experience, JUNO brings clear improvements in how release testing is handled:

- Makes the release testing process more structured and easier to manage across different modules
- Significantly reduces manual effort by enabling test execution through natural language and centralized monitoring
- Ensures consistent and repeatable testing, helping avoid missed validations during release cycles
- Minimizes the risk of critical issues by improving test coverage and visibility
- Improves collaboration between HR and IT teams through clear and accessible execution insights
- Runs securely on SAP Business Technology Platform and scales effectively across multiple modules
- In my experience, this also improved overall confidence during release cycles, as teams had better visibility and control over test execution

Conclusion

Release testing in SAP SuccessFactors is critical, but managing it can become complex with frequent updates and multiple dependencies.

From what I’ve seen in real projects, adding an intelligent layer like JUNO Joule Agent completely changes the way teams approach testing. Instead of worrying about tools, scripts, or execution steps, teams can directly focus on validating business scenarios.

For me, the biggest impact was the significant reduction in manual effort, improved clarity across the testing process, and overall smoother and faster release cycles.

This approach not only improves efficiency but also changes how teams think about release testing—from a technical task to a more business-focused validation process.

entry.title: Formal Name Not Just a Field
entry.content:
What is the Name Format feature?

The Name Format is a feature that concatenates name fields based on your specific configuration. Additionally, it allows you to define different concatenation rules for each legal entity.

For example, you can set a format like First Name + Preferred Name + Last Name specifically for a Legal Entity in Germany, while using Last Name + First Name for one in Japan.

How do I enable the Name Format feature?

To enable this, you must select "Enable Name Format" under "Company Settings" in Provisioning.

 

How is the concatenation configured?

The concatenation logic is defined within the Name Format object. (Manage Data) 

How do I assign a Name Format to a company?

You assign the Name Format through the Legal Entity object.

If you don't see the "Name Format for Legal Entity" is because you have not implemented the feature yet. You hace to associate the object to the "Legal Entity" object KBA.

Where is the concatenation displayed?

Once all the configuration is complete, the formal-name field (personal-info) is the one that will reflect the name concatenation you have defined. This ensures that the system automatically populates the name in the specific format required by your company.

 

Where will I see the Formal Name concatenation?

The configured format directly impacts the Formal Name field within the personalInfo entity. Furthermore, this is the field that populates the information displayed on Quickcards.

Does this Name Format affect the "General Display Name"?

Yes. The General Display Name (GDN) is an SAP feature designed to show employee names consistently across the entire SAP SuccessFactors HXM Suite. Once the "Name Format" (in manage data) is configured, that specific concatenation is what will be reflected in the GDN feature (additional configuration is required).  For more detailed information, you can refer to the official SAP blog post: Adopting General Display Name.

In Summary

entry.title: 1H2026 Release: A journey to Autonomous Payroll with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content:
Hello Everyone!

To advance our vision of an autonomous payroll, we are introducing four specialized Payroll Agents for the SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) solution in this first half 2026 release.

You can now benefit from automations across 1) payroll and time explanations, 2) alert resolution, 3) rule creation, and 4) data integration. These AI agents detect, explain, and resolve issues before they impact your workforce. They streamline how payroll teams prepare and run payroll by orchestrating checks across time, workflow, and payroll controls.

Below are the key highlights, but be sure to explore the What's New Viewer for the complete release notes and full details on everything we’re delivering.

Payroll Explanation Agent

With this update, the Payroll Explanation Agent connects Payroll and Time sheet data into one unified explanation, giving employees a clear, easy‑to‑understand view of their pay statements and the time data behind them. Employees get crisp, clear and fast answers without relying on HR. This enhances employee experience, builds trust and confidence in payroll accuracy and lowers operational costs. PostNL, one of our customers based in Netherlands, expects 80% cost reduction in handling payroll questions with an AI agent. Additionally, support now extends to Brazil and Canada, building on the 2025 release in the USA, UK, Australia, Mexico, the Netherlands, and Germany.

  • See the Payroll Explanation Agent in action here (video)

 

[Screenshot] Payroll Explanation Agent now integrates Time sheet data and supports Brazil and Canada

Payroll Alert Resolution Agent

This agent streamlines how payroll administrators manage Payroll Control Center (PCC) alerts by turning complex investigations into a guided, intelligent workflow. It explains why alerts occur, identifies the most likely root causes, and recommends prioritized fixes directly within the payroll control center. Administrators can also create new validation rules using natural‑language through a conversational interface.

  • See the Payroll Alert Resolution Agent in action here (video)

[Screenshot] Payroll Alert Resolution Agent in ECP

Payroll Validation Rule Agent

This rule creation agent introduces a conversational, AI‑assisted way for payroll administrators to build new validation rules directly within the Payroll Control Center. Instead of navigating technical configuration steps, admins simply describe the rule they need in natural language.

Instead of navigating technical configuration steps, admins can simply describe the rule they need in natural language making the whole process of validation rule creation 50% faster.

[Screenshot] Payroll Validation Rule Agent in ECP

Employee Data Integration Agent

The agent helps payroll administrator resolve payroll replication errors by translating complex technical messages into clear, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central–focused guidance. It identifies impacted EC fields based on ECP mappings and provides step‑by‑step recommendations for fixing data issues. This release addresses manual corrections.

For example, a missing cost center for an employee. Instead of leaving the admin to interpret a technical error message, the agent analyzes organizational data and suggests the most likely cost center—based on where the employee’s peers in the same department and reporting to the same manager are assigned.

The result: cleaner data, fewer payroll interruptions, and far less time spent resolving issues after the fact.

  • See the Employee Data Integration Agent in action here (video)

[Screenshot] Employee Data Integration Agent in ECP

 

What Else is New this Release?

There are a number of additional innovations available for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll in the first half 2026 release, including

  • Enhancements to Payroll Control Center: Manage Payroll Activities
  • Enhanced Gender Field Replication from Employee Central to Employee Central Payroll
  • Dozens of legal changes for specific country/region (documented in detail in the Regulatory Change Manager)
  • And more

See the What's New Viewer for more information on these features.

Where Can I Find More Information?

Here are additional resources for those interested in learning more about SAP Payroll solutions

  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Website
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll SAP Help (Implementation) (Requires ID)
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Community Page (Discussion Forum) (Requires ID)
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Learning Journey (Educational Content) (Requires SAP Lea...
  • Other recent release highlights for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
    • 2H/2025 Release Highlight Blog
    • 1H/2025 Release Highlight Blog
    • 2H/2024 Release Highlight Blog
    • 1H/2024 Release Highlight Blog

For information on release highlights across SAP SuccessFactors HCM, visit this one stop shop for all product release and road map information

entry.title: How We Built ASTRA Joule Agent for Automation Testing Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM
entry.content:
Introduction

In my experience working on multiple SAP SuccessFactors HCM implementations, one phase that consistently stands out as challenging is testing. No matter how well the system is designed, testing often becomes time-consuming and difficult to manage—especially when dealing with multiple modules like Employee Central, Payroll, and integrations.

During several projects, I observed that teams were spending a significant amount of effort on repetitive manual validation, coordination across systems, and analyzing failures. While automation tools helped to some extent, they still required heavy setup and maintenance.

This led us to explore how we could make testing more efficient and intelligent using SAP BTP and AI capabilities. That’s how we started working on what we now call the ASTRA Joule HR Agent—a solution aimed at simplifying and improving the testing experience.

The Challenge We Faced

From a practical standpoint, testing in SAP SuccessFactors is not just about executing scripts—it involves validating complete business processes and ensuring data consistency across systems.

Some of the common challenges we encountered were:

  • Handling large regression cycles with hundreds of scenarios
  • Managing complex and realistic test data
  • Difficulty in identifying the root cause of failures
  • Constant switching between multiple tools (SuccessFactors, test tools, Jira, etc.)

These issues often slowed down release cycles and required significant manual effort.

Our Approach – ASTRA Joule HR Agent

To address these gaps, we built ASTRA as a centralized, intelligent powerhouse on SAP Business Technology Platform. We leveraged SAP Business AI through Joule skills to create a conversational, "human-centric" testing experience.

Instead of navigating complex menus, users can interact with ASTRA through SAP Build Work Zone. By integrating Joule, we’ve made it possible to manage tests, gain insights into failures, and even trigger executions using natural language.

Key Technical Highlights:

  • Prebuilt Library: We launched with 926+ automation scripts aligned with SAP Best Practices.

  • Full-Stack Integration: Built using the SAP Cloud Application Programming  model, ensuring seamless OData connectivity with SuccessFactors and S/4HANA.

  • AI Orchestration: Using Joule to provide smarter interactions and faster insights into test logs.

Benefits

1. Extensive Module Coverage

ASTRA doesn’t just "test"; it validates the entire HCM lifecycle. This includes Employee Central, Recruiting, Onboarding, Benefits, Time Management, and Performance & Goals. It even handles custom Extension Center objects, ensuring your unique business logic is never left unchecked.

 

 

2. End-to-End & Integration Testing - Enables both functional and integration testing across SAP SuccessFactors, SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA, and external systems—helping ensure full end-to-end process validation.

 

 

 

 

 

 

3. AI-Powered Insights (Joule Skills)

By utilizing SAP Business AI, ASTRA helps users understand why a test failed without digging through raw logs. Joule assists in identifying failed scenarios and provides intelligent summaries, drastically reducing investigation time.

 

 

 

 

4. Dynamic, Input-Driven Execution

Efficiency is key. ASTRA allows users to enter specific Entity IDs to run focused tests for individual records. The system dynamically filters and executes only the relevant test cases based on your input data.

 

 

 

5. Automated Documentation & Defect Management

  • Traceability: Every execution generates a PDF-based result document for step-by-step audit trails.

  • Jira Integration: Failed scenarios automatically trigger ticket creation in JIRA, ensuring the engineering team is notified in real-time.

 

 

 

6. Joule-Powered Assistance with SAP Business AI (Joule Skills)

  • Uses SAP Business AI through Joule skills to enhance test execution and analysis.
  • Provides intelligent assistance to help users understand test results and failures faster.
  • Helps identify failed scenarios and related logs without manual analysis.
  • Improves overall testing efficiency by reducing time spent on investigation and coordination.

 

 

Business Value

From what I’ve observed while working with this approach, the impact is quite practical and noticeable in day-to-day project execution:

  • Faster Release Cycles: With better visibility into test results and fewer manual dependencies, we were able to move through release cycles with more confidence and less delay.
  • Reduced Manual Effort: A significant portion of repetitive validation was minimized, which allowed both functional and technical teams to spend more time on critical tasks rather than regression testing.
  • Improved Data Reliability: By validating end-to-end scenarios with realistic test data, we could identify issues earlier and avoid unexpected problems during go-live.
  • Better Visibility for Stakeholders: Having centralized dashboards within SAP Build Work Zone made it easier for stakeholders to track progress, understand results, and make informed decisions without relying on multiple tools.

Conclusion

Based on my experience, testing in SAP SuccessFactors projects often becomes a coordination-heavy activity. What worked well for us with this approach was shifting the focus from managing test execution to actually improving system quality.

By leveraging capabilities available on SAP Business Technology Platform along with Joule, we were able to make testing more streamlined and easier to manage.

The ASTRA Joule HR Agent, as we implemented it, is not just about automation—it’s about making testing more meaningful, reducing effort, and helping teams deliver stable and reliable HR solutions more consistently.

entry.title: Enhanced WorkFlow Notifications (Pending Data) - SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Introduction :

The Workflows always missed the necessary context in the Header and needed to be expanded or navigated through the link to look into the content. Workflow approvers always wanted critical data in the email notification , to decide on the priority to act on the workflow. This was a ask from Business for a while now, which was not feasible until now. Thanks to 1H 2026 Release , with the new release , Pending Data can now be mapped to the fields in the Document Generation Template and critical data can now be made visible in the email notification.

Enhanced Workflow Notifications :

The Object used here for the Configuration is Benefit Employee Claim which was the most asked from the Businesses.

Step 1 : Create a Document Generation Group

Document Generation - Manage Document Generation Template -> Create New -> Document Generation Group

Step 2 : Create a Document Generation Template

Document Generation - Manage Document Generation Template -> Create New -> Document Generation Template

Step 3 : Document Generation Template Mapping

The Critical Part of the configuration which does the Magic!

Notice the Reference (Pending Data) , As part of the Release SAP has introduced a new reference type called Pending Data which then allows you to select the Base Object and the field to be mapped.

Step 4 : Custom Workflow Configurations 

Custom Workflow Configuration is another critical aspect of the configuration.

Manage Workflow Email Configuration and Create a New Group 

Map the Document Generation Group to the Workflow Actions as per your Business Needs.

Step 5 : Assignment in Workflow

Assign the custom workflow under Workflow Email Configuration  

The Email Notification :

Raise a new Request (Reimbursement in this case) , and the Workflow Notification is sent with the most critical data required for the approvers to view and decide at a glance.

 

 

OLDEnhanced Workflow Notification
Create Benefit Employee Claim NotificationPurchase Claim Pending Approval

Notice the highlighted text in the Table and see how a Little context addition makes so much difference to the Workflow notification.

What used to be a usual typical workflow email notification is now enhanced with critical and necessary data. This is not limited to Benefit Employee Claim and can be used with any Custom MDF which would be the most used scenario of the Enhanced Workflow Notification.

Conclusion

What was once a basic workflow email notification is now transformed into a more informative and actionable communication. By surfacing critical data directly within the notification, approvers can make quicker and more informed decisions. This definitely will turn out as one of the most used features of the Employee Central.

entry.title: First Half 2026 Release: What's New in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central?
entry.content:
As organizations continue to navigate evolving workforce expectations and increasingly complex regulatory environments, HR teams need a strong and reliable core that keeps people processes moving while maintaining data accuracy and compliance. With the First Half 2026 release, we are introducing new innovations in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central that help reduce friction in daily HR operations, improve data quality, and support scalable global HR practices.

Alongside foundational core HR enhancements, this release introduces AI agents in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central. These agents are designed to help monitor workflows and data, surface potential issues, and support faster resolution. Let’s take a closer look at the innovations coming with this release.

Faster, More Informed Approvals with Enhanced Workflow Notifications

Approvals are a critical moment in core HR processes, yet approvers often lack the full context needed to act quickly and confidently. This release enhances workflow notifications by bringing pending, to‑be‑approved employee data directly into the notification itself. Instead of navigating into the system to review details, approvers receive the information they need upfront.

Using field mapping, administrators can now define variables for these notifications to include pending (to-be-approved) data, providing approvers with the key context they need directly in email. This added transparency reduces back‑and‑forth, accelerates decision‑making, and helps keep HR processes moving smoothly, particularly in fast‑paced or high‑volume environments.

Keeping Workflows Moving with the Pending Workflow Agent

Even with improved notifications, workflows can still stall when requests go unnoticed or approvers are unavailable. This release introduces the Pending Workflow Agent, which is designed to surface workflow status and help keep tasks on track by proactively identifying and addressing bottlenecks.

The Pending Workflow Agent monitors how long requests have been pending and evaluates approver availability to determine when action is needed. It can nudge approvers or reroute requests to delegates or higher‑level managers to avoid delays. This capability is designed to help reduce time‑to‑resolution by up to 40 percent, while minimizing administrative effort and lowering compliance risk.

                                                                          Pending Workflow Agent 

Improving Data Accuracy with the Employee Data Integration Agent

Accurate and consistent employee data is essential, particularly when supporting payroll and other downstream processes. A new Employee Data Integration Agent enables organizations to detect data inconsistencies across core HR and payroll systems before they create downstream impact.

The agent identifies discrepancies, proposes fixes, and applies approved changes to help keep employee records accurate and aligned across systems. By proactively addressing data issues, organizations can reduce manual rework, avoid payroll disruptions, and maintain a more reliable system of record.

Check out this demo to see the agent in action: Demo: Data Integration Agent

             Data Integration Agent

Simplifying Global Compliance with Country‑ and Region‑Specific Dependents Management

Managing dependents data across countries requires balancing global consistency with local legal requirements. This release introduces enhancements to dependents management to simplify updates for employees and admins while supporting compliance.

Organizations can define country‑ or region‑specific dependent types, objects, and required data fields to meet local legal requirements. Out‑of‑the‑box country settings enable faster implementation, while configuration can be easily modified through a single, unified user interface. By eliminating the need for complicated business rules, HR teams can maintain compliant dependent data more efficiently, while employees benefit from a clearer and more consistent self‑service experience. Furthermore, optimized data management ensures accurate information flows to downstream processes including payroll, benefits, and time management. 

                 Dependents management enhancements

Strengthening the Intelligent HR Core

With these delivered innovations in the 1H 2026 release, we continue to strengthen SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central as the intelligent HR core. Enhancements to approvals, the introduction of AI agents to help monitor workflows and data, and simplified localized compliance all enable more efficient operations today and establish a strong foundation for connected core HR, payroll, and talent processes across SAP SuccessFactors HCM.

Get Started with the 1H 2026 Release

  • Register for the First Half 2026 Customer Release Q&A session for Employee Central to hear directly from SAP experts
  • Explore the What's New Viewer for a complete overview of all SAP SuccessFactors First Half 2026 innovations
  • Visit the Product Release & Road Map Information page to access additional resources, including release assets and product road maps

entry.title: Effective Start Date Validation in Payment Information - SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Introduction :

Every time after the payroll run, One of my Clients faced a recurring issue and this recurring issue was causing last minute hassles and inconvenience to the Business. The Issue is the maintenance of Bank Details in the Payment Information Portlet mid of the month by the HR Team as they don't notice the effective start date which by defaults sets to today. Maintenance of mid-month Bank details will impact all the payroll reports, and the reports will show inconsistent values , which will then impact reconciliation and so on..

How a simple and effective Business Rule solved this challenge?

I noticed this issue become so common over the days and as a consultant it's my core duty to understand and resolve the business pain point. So I configured a simple and effective rule which will solve this recurring issue and any issues that follow by.

The Business Rule :

Base Object : Payment Information

Purpose : Evaluate / Validate

This simple rule here does the magic of validating the effective start and throwing error message when maintained mid of the month. There's a little catch and purpose behind using the previous effective validation here , the reason is explained below

We don't have an option in Business Rule for MDF's to have it triggered only for certain scenarios which means this will also trigger in the new hire UI. Imagine an employee joining mid of the month 03rd May 2026 , the validation kicks and throws error , ID is skipped and issue occurs.

To avoid this rule being triggered for the new hires , Previous Effective dated record is not equal to null condition is added in the IF condition.

Conclusion

This simple validation rule effectively prevents mid-month changes to bank details, eliminating inconsistencies in payroll reports and reducing reconciliation issues.

It also highlights an important lesson:
Every validation or enhancement should be carefully designed with edge cases in mind and thoroughly tested across all scenarios.

entry.title: Latest People Profile Enablement: What You Need to Know
entry.content:
Introduction

This blog explains the points to be noted, key configurations to be done, and behaviours observed during the migration from the legacy People Profile to the latest People Profile.

Legacy People Profile to Latest People Profile Migration

Migration process:

To migrate configurations from the Legacy People Profile to the Latest People Profile: 

  • Navigate to Company System and Logo Settings >> Select “Migrate Legacy People Profile Configurations” >> Run the migration job  

With the 1H 2026 release, the Latest People Profile is automatically enabled, and the migration job is executed by default. However, administrators can still trigger the job manually if required. This change cannot be reverted to the Legacy People Profile.

Card Arrangement After Migration:

After the migration job is completed, both predefined and custom cards are reorganized into structured categories.

Predefined category:

Custom Category:

  • Custom blocks such as Live Profile Background Information, MDF Information, User Information, and Notes are moved to the Full Profile as custom cards.
  • These cards are organized into custom categories, keeping the same structure as in the old (legacy) profile layout.

System Limitations in Latest People Profile :

  • Maximum 20 custom categories allowed  
  • Maximum 30 cards per category  

Setting Category Access Control in Latest People Profile:

By default, the latest People Profile categories are visible to everyone. To restrict visibility based on roles, change the access to Role-Based Permissions.

Edit each category and configure the category access control.

 

Aligning Employee View Permissions Between Legacy and Latest People Profile

Navigate to Manage Permission Roles → Edit Role → Employee Views.

Under Employee Views, two categories are available:

  • People Profile Categories – used for the latest People Profile
  • Employee View Sections (only for Legacy People Profile) – used for the legacy People Profile

Refer to the permissions defined under Employee View Sections (only for Legacy People Profile) and provide similar permissions in People Profile Categories for each role.

You can also generate a report to review the list of roles and their existing legacy Employee View permissions and use it as a reference.

Set Header Fields in Latest People Profile:

During migration from legacy to the latest People Profile, header fields will not be migrated. They must be configured again in Configure Latest People Profile → General Settings → Configure Header Fields.

Common Issues and Behaviours in Latest People Profile Configuration:

  1. If there are duplicate sections or duplicate objects in the legacy People Profile during migration to the latest People Profile, only the first occurrence is considered, and the subsequent ones will not be displayed.
  2. Non-secured objects will not be displayed in the latest People Profile. For example, Advances may initially not be visible, so ensure the security for both Non-Recurring Payments and Advances objects is set to “Yes” in Configure Object Definitions.
  3. If the default screen in Configure Object Definition for objects to be displayed in the Employee Profile is not set, then even if the object permission is not granted to the employee, the card will still be displayed in their profile with an error. So, make sure to set it.
  4. If the compensation pie chart is not displayed in the employee’s Profile Compensation Information, it is because the employee does not have permission for the Pay Component Group.
  5. Recurring Deduction – even if permission is not granted, the card is displayed when there is no data. However, once data is entered, the card disappears, but there is no functional impact.
  6. If the Compensation Statement card is not visible in the Employee Profile, ensure that the necessary permission is granted for that in employee data.
  7. If any custom objects are set inside standard sections like Job Information, Compensation Information, etc., in the legacy People Profile, they will disappear during the latest People Profile migration and need to be set again in the relevant categories in the latest People Profile.
  8. In the legacy People Profile, the Org Chart was available under General Information. In the latest People Profile, the Org Chart is now available under Actions.

Conclusion

By May 15,16, 2026, the Latest People Profile will be automatically enabled in production. Therefore, ensure that all necessary configurations are completed, and the required permissions are granted.

Make sure to review legacy configurations and migrate any required settings to the latest People Profile categories. Validate role-based permissions to avoid missing or restricted visibility of cards and sections. Also, test the profile view to ensure all relevant data is displayed correctly after migration.

entry.title: SAP Sapphire 2026: Unlocking Next-Level Innovation to AI with SAP Business AI Platform
entry.content:
*Updated on May 15 with SAP Business AI Platform

Hello SAP Community!

Mark your calendars – SAP Sapphire 2026 is here, and it's set to redefine what's possible. This year, the conversation isn’t just about digital transformation. The focus is on mastering your platform journey to drive continuous innovation, achieve measurable business outcomes, and on how organizations should leverage artificial intelligence. It’s going to be a fantastic opportunity to learn, share together, and of course, I’m especially looking forward to celebrating with everyone at the afterparty. I just found out there’s hazelnut chocolate fruit waffles at Magic Kingdom, so I’m extra excited!

From AI-powered development and intelligent automation to seamless integration and strategic platform management, SAP’s Business AI platform (SAP BAIP) stands as the essential foundation. Its unique differentiation lies in deeply embedding AI across your operations while preserving the invaluable data and business application context, thereby turning challenges into tangible opportunities. Sapphire 2026 will be the pivotal event to launch SAP’s strategic vision, highlighting how AI innovations are integral to the enterprises of tomorrow.

 

The Mandate: Connecting Everything, Across the Entire Landscape

In an era when disconnected landscapes continue to hinder progress, SAP’s customers have consistently shown that the platform's true power lies in end-to-end connectivity. Whether it's your CHRO leveraging SAP SuccessFactors employee data with procurement data for newly onboarding employees, or your CFO optimizing financials with SAP S/4HANA together with external third-party benchmark data, or your CPO driving procurement excellence by keeping warehouse data up to date from supply chain partners, the challenge remains: how do you connect these vital functions, including between the AI agents, to move beyond implementation to true business transformation?

This year, you’ll see how SAP Business AI platform serves as the critical foundation for SAP’s business applications, making this connectivity a reality. It's designed to streamline operations, foster innovation, and enable real-time analysis of insights across your entire business landscape.

 

Spotlight on Transformation: Breakthrough Use Cases for a Connected Future

This Sapphire, we're particularly excited to unveil new use cases that exemplify the SAP Business AI platform’s power to address today's most pressing business challenges.

 

1. Integrate HR data and processes with government and third‑party systems

  • The Challenge: Navigating complex and evolving HR regulations, increasing demands for efficiency in HR service delivery, and managing diverse workforces effectively.
  • How the SAP BAIP helps: By integrating HR data in SAP SuccessFactors (or elsewhere) and processes with government and third-party systems through SAP Integration Suite, organizations can ensure compliance with global and local regulations, automate processes, and streamline interactions with third-parties. This connected process frees the HR department from administrative burdens, creating streamlined employee experiences and compliant data.
  • Recommended sessions at Sapphire:
    • Proving the business value of standardizing on SAP Integration Suite | BTP2350: Hear how teams quantify business value after standardizing on SAP Integration Suite: before-and-after metrics, faster time to integrate, fewer incidents, lower operating effort, and readiness for a mixed SAP and third-party software landscape. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • PPG Industries: Building an AI-ready, clean core integration foundation | BTP2638: See how APIs, events, and cloud integration reduce complexity, improve visibility, and establish an AI-ready foundation for future innovation across SAP and third-party systems. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • Building a foundation for AI with an integrated core HR system | HCM1742: Learn how a connected approach to core HR, payroll, workforce management, and HR service delivery can help you streamline your people and business processes while enabling AI readiness. 
      🗓️ADD NOW

2. Drive profitable growth with secure, personalized digital experiences

  • The Challenge: Organizations struggle with complex processes and fragmented access to customer data, facing increasing competition and rising customer expectations. Data silos often lead to slow and reactive decision-making, impacting customer satisfaction and growth.
  • How SAP BAIP helps: SAP Customer Identity and Access Management (SAP CIAM) protects sensitive customer data and meets global compliance requirements. By consolidating and integrating customer data across various systems with SAP’s platform, it enables the creation of secure, personalized digital experiences that overcome data silos. This supports dynamic and tailored customer interactions, enhancing customer acquisition and retention.
  • Recommended sessions at Sapphire:
    • Standardize, secure, and scale with core platform capabilities | BTP1315: Learn how application lifecycle management, security, and interoperability unlock SAP’s Platform to drive agility and growth. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • Invisible security: SAP Customer Identity and Access Management | BTP1316: Discover how SAP Customer Identity and Access Management accelerates your business by harnessing AI-assisted risk analysis to stop threats in real time, protecting customers behind the scenes without disrupting experiences. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • Secure by design: How SAP is redefining agent execution | BTP2641: Learn how SAP is developing the secure execution layer, combining runtime containment with the governance of AI-enabled data fabric solutions from SAP to define production-ready agentic AI.
      🗓️🗓ADD NOW

 

3. Leverage AI Capabilities in ABAP Cloud Development

  • The Challenge: Developers struggle to balance rapid delivery with maintaining code standards, while traditional coding, debugging, and testing consume a significant portion of their time. Complex legacy code also slows productivity.
  • How SAP BAIP helps: Leveraging AI capabilities directly within ABAP Cloud Development, using tools like SAP Joule for Developers, can dramatically increase developer productivity. AI assists with code generation, ensures code quality, and helps your development teams understand complex systems, freeing them to focus on innovation.
  • Recommended sessions at Sapphire:
    • Agentic ABAP with SAP Joule for Developers: Build faster, migrate smarter | JOU1428: Find out how AI agents with ABAP simplify migration, speed up coding, and increase productivity, even in older versions of SAP S/4HANA. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • ABAP Cloud: Entering the new era of ABAP development | BTP2107: The ABAP Cloud development model, agentic development experience in VS Code, the SAP Joule for Developers add-on, and agentic custom code migration join forces for the next era of growth in ABAP development tools. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • Introduction to ABAP Cloud supported by generative AI | BTP1429: Learn what ABAP Cloud is, get insights into the latest technology advancements, and see how generative AI boosts your adoption of ABAP Cloud. 
      🗓️ADD NOW

 

4. Modernize On-Premise Integration to Cloud with PI/PO to SAP Integration Suite Migration

  • The Challenge: Organizations still using SAP Process Integration/Process Orchestration (SAP PI/PO) are facing the impending need to modernize the integration methods. With a complex integration landscape comprising multiple integration solutions, not only does it add complexity, but it also creates greater technical debt.
  • How SAP BAIP helps: Migrating from SAP PI/PO to SAP Integration Suite provides a modern, cloud-native platform that reduces Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) across the integration landscape. This transition can cut the time to set up new interfaces and unlock significant annual cost savings. It's a strategic move that not only streamlines operations but also provides the scalable, agile integration backbone essential for future AI-driven cloud transformation.
  • Recommended sessions at Sapphire:
    • Modernize your strategy: Migrating from PI/PO to SAP Integration Suite | BTP1360: Learn how the suite enables smooth, scalable, and intelligent integrations across cloud and hybrid landscapes. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • Under Armour: Enabling real-time commerce with SAP Integration Suite | BTP2138: Under Armour is consolidating MuleSoft, Boomi, and SAP Process Integration and SAP Process Orchestration into SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • Modernize from SAP Process Integration and SAP Process Orchestration | BTP2100: Learn about migration approaches, coexistence options, and governance—plus the benefits customers see after moving, such as faster change cycles and better reliability and monitoring.
      🗓️ADD NOW

 

Mastering Your SAP Business AI Platform Journey: Measure, Optimize, and Guide


To truly maximize your investment in SAP BAIP, it’s crucial to understand where your organization currently stands and how to strategically advance. Sapphire 2026 will highlight the tools and methodologies designed to guide this journey, available on SAP Discovery Center: 

  • SAP Discovery Center: As your central hub for knowledge and hands-on learning, the Discovery Center continues to evolve. Discover, evaluate, and adopt SAP’s Platform and AI to create tailored business solutions and accelerate your platform adoption.

  • Maturity Assessment: Introduced at last year’s SAP TechEd, this comprehensive technical evaluation transforms uncertainty into actionable insights. In just 10-15 minutes, you can get a precise understanding of your organization's platform maturity across five levels, from "Getting Started" to "Center of Excellence." It provides a clear roadmap, justifies further investments, and uncovers hidden efficiencies and innovation opportunities.

We have since expanded the portfolio with new Line of Business AI assessments for domain-specific insights, and an AI Maturity Calculator is in development with IDC that not only assesses your platform AI readiness but also benchmarks you against your industry peers.

Participants will explore the BTP ecosystem, from architecture and development frameworks to enterprise integration and data management solutions.

Your Strategic Next Steps at Sapphire 2026: Attend Must-Attend Sessions:

This Sapphire, don't just attend – Seize this opportunity to proactively empower your organization by understanding the strategic future of AI. In addition to use-case specific recommendations, I’ve identified essential sessions to deepen your strategic understanding.

Secure your spot: add these pivotal sessions to your agenda to fully grasp SAP’s AI and platform vision for enterprise innovation.

    🗓️ADD NOW

    🗓️ADD NOW
  • SAP's AI platform: What's new, what's next, and why it matters | BTP1448: Get a tour of the latest innovations, hands‑on tools for developers and business users, and an early preview of upcoming capabilities shaping the future of AI on the platform. 
    🗓️ADD NOW
  • Design the future of enterprise AI agents with Joule Studio | BTP1449: Discover how Joule Studio enables teams to shape the next era of intelligent enterprises. With an intuitive no‑code experience, create secure, context‑aware AI agents that plan, reason, and act across SAP solutions and beyond.
    🗓️ADD NOW

See you there!

Sapphire 2026 is your opportunity to discover how SAP Business AI Platform can propel your organization forward to AI adoption, helping you become a truly connected enterprise with governance, ready for whatever the future holds.
